title: Freight Rail Car Utilization Collapse Signal
thesis: When freight rail car utilization drops sharply week-over-week, it signals inventory drawdown and reduced industrial demand, which precedes market softness. Rail utilization is a leading indicator of manufacturing and goods production momentum.
data_sources: Association of American Railroads weekly freight car utilization through html_table adapter
signal_logic: If weekly rail car utilization falls more than 3% from prior week and is below its 8-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly rail car utilization falls more than 3% from prior week and is below its 8-week moving average Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if utilization rebounds above 8-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Association of American Railroads weekly freight car utilization through html_table adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rail car utilization swings 2-4% weekly; cyclical movements are frequent enough to generate 3-4 setups per quarter.

schema.idea: Freight Rail Car Utilization Collapse Signal

data.source: Association of American Railroads weekly freight car utilization through html_table adapter

schema.behavior: When freight rail car utilization drops sharply week-over-week, it signals inventory drawdown and reduced industrial demand, which precedes market softness.

market_impact.rationale: Rail utilization is a leading indicator of manufacturing and goods production momentum.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly rail car utilization falls more than 3% from prior week and is below its 8-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if utilization rebounds above 8-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rail car utilization swings 2-4% weekly; cyclical movements are frequent enough to generate 3-4 setups per quarter.

title: Ev Charger Network Density Surge Expansion Signal
thesis: Rapid regional EV charger deployment signals localized infrastructure spending and confidence in electric vehicle adoption, which correlates with discretionary sector strength. EV charger expansion indicates consumer and regional economic confidence in green mobility transition.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap EV charger location count by region through openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: If week-over-week increase in public charger count exceeds 2% and is above 4-week rolling average growth rate
entry_exit: Entry: If week-over-week increase in public charger count exceeds 2% and is above 4-week rolling average growth rate Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if charger growth rate falls below 4-week average for 2 weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap EV charger location count by region through openchargemap adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger counts update weekly; sustained national expansion creates multiple regional surge events per month.

schema.idea: EV Charger Network Density Surge Expansion Signal

data.source: OpenChargeMap EV charger location count by region through openchargemap adapter

schema.behavior: Rapid regional EV charger deployment signals localized infrastructure spending and confidence in electric vehicle adoption, which correlates with discretionary sector strength.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger expansion indicates consumer and regional economic confidence in green mobility transition.

trade_logic.entry: If week-over-week increase in public charger count exceeds 2% and is above 4-week rolling average growth rate
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if charger growth rate falls below 4-week average for 2 weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger counts update weekly; sustained national expansion creates multiple regional surge events per month.

title: Google Trends Surge In Shipping Delay Complaints
thesis: Spikes in Google searches for shipping delays signal consumer frustration with logistics and supply chain friction, indicating short-term discretionary spending anxiety. Consumer frustration with delivery quality often precedes pullback in e-commerce and discretionary purchases.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'package delivery late' and 'shipping delay' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly search volume for shipping delay terms rises 40% week-over-week and exceeds 52-week median by 50%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search volume for shipping delay terms rises 40% week-over-week and exceeds 52-week median by 50% Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if search volume falls below 52-week median for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'package delivery late' and 'shipping delay' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal peaks and logistics disruptions create 3-5 high-volume events per quarter in search data.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'package delivery late' and 'shipping delay' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in Google searches for shipping delays signal consumer frustration with logistics and supply chain friction, indicating short-term discretionary spending anxiety.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer frustration with delivery quality often precedes pullback in e-commerce and discretionary purchases.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search volume for shipping delay terms rises 40% week-over-week and exceeds 52-week median by 50%
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if search volume falls below 52-week median for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal peaks and logistics disruptions create 3-5 high-volume events per quarter in search data.

title: Fred Labor Force Participation Cliff Drop
thesis: Sharp week-over-week drops in labor force participation signal sudden workforce exits (illness, discouragement, early retirement), foreshadowing demand weakness. Labor force participation drops precede consumer weakness and reduced discretionary spending capacity.
data_sources: FRED series CIVPART (Civilian Labor Force Participation Rate) weekly through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If CIVPART drops more than 0.2% from prior week and is below its 12-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If CIVPART drops more than 0.2% from prior week and is below its 12-week moving average Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if CIVPART rebounds above 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series CIVPART (Civilian Labor Force Participation Rate) weekly through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and cyclical swings in participation create 3-4 entry signals per quarter.

data.source: FRED series CIVPART (Civilian Labor Force Participation Rate) weekly through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: Sharp week-over-week drops in labor force participation signal sudden workforce exits (illness, discouragement, early retirement), foreshadowing demand weakness.

market_impact.rationale: Labor force participation drops precede consumer weakness and reduced discretionary spending capacity.

trade_logic.entry: If CIVPART drops more than 0.2% from prior week and is below its 12-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if CIVPART rebounds above 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and cyclical swings in participation create 3-4 entry signals per quarter.

title: Earthquake Activity Surge Insurance Cost Proxy
thesis: Clusters of seismic activity signal increased natural disaster risk, which drives up insurance and reinsurance costs, pressuring financial sector margins. Insurance and reinsurance cost spikes directly reduce financial sector profitability.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity (magnitude 4.0+) daily count by week through earthquake_activity adapter
signal_logic: If weekly count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes doubles from prior week's 4-week average and exceeds historical 75th percentile
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes doubles from prior week's 4-week average and exceeds historical 75th percentile Exit: Exit after 12 trading days or if weekly earthquake count falls below prior 4-week average for 2 weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity (magnitude 4.0+) daily count by week through earthquake_activity adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic clustering is geologically common; 2-3 surge periods occur per quarter on average.

schema.idea: Earthquake Activity Surge Insurance Cost Proxy

data.source: USGS earthquake activity (magnitude 4.0+) daily count by week through earthquake_activity adapter

schema.behavior: Clusters of seismic activity signal increased natural disaster risk, which drives up insurance and reinsurance costs, pressuring financial sector margins.

market_impact.rationale: Insurance and reinsurance cost spikes directly reduce financial sector profitability.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes doubles from prior week's 4-week average and exceeds historical 75th percentile
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 12 trading days or if weekly earthquake count falls below prior 4-week average for 2 weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic clustering is geologically common; 2-3 surge periods occur per quarter on average.

title: Weather-driven Natural Gas Demand Shock
thesis: Extreme temperature swings cause sudden demand spikes for heating/cooling energy, driving natural gas and utilities prices higher in the short term. Extreme weather directly increases utility demand and pricing power for the utility sector.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly (deviation from 30-year normal) through weather_series adapter
signal_logic: If daily temperature anomaly exceeds ±15°F from normal for a region and is at 90th+ percentile of historical distribution, close position if anomaly persists for 5+ days
entry_exit: Entry: If daily temperature anomaly exceeds ±15°F from normal for a region and is at 90th+ percentile of historical distribution, close position if anomaly persists for 5+ days Exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if temperature returns within ±5°F of normal for 3 consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly (deviation from 30-year normal) through weather_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal extremes (winter cold snaps, summer heat waves) produce multiple events per month.

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly (deviation from 30-year normal) through weather_series adapter

schema.behavior: Extreme temperature swings cause sudden demand spikes for heating/cooling energy, driving natural gas and utilities prices higher in the short term.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme weather directly increases utility demand and pricing power for the utility sector.

trade_logic.entry: If daily temperature anomaly exceeds ±15°F from normal for a region and is at 90th+ percentile of historical distribution, close position if anomaly persists for 5+ days
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if temperature returns within ±5°F of normal for 3 consecutive days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal extremes (winter cold snaps, summer heat waves) produce multiple events per month.

title: Healthcare Search Cost Crisis Panic Index
thesis: Spikes in healthcare cost and medical debt searches signal consumer panic over healthcare expenses, which depresses discretionary spending and increases financial stress. Healthcare cost shock reduces disposable income and confidence in consumer discretionary purchases.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical debt' and 'hospital bill payment plan' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly search volume for medical debt/bill payment terms rises 50% week-over-week and exceeds 52-week median by 60%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search volume for medical debt/bill payment terms rises 50% week-over-week and exceeds 52-week median by 60% Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if search volume falls below 52-week median for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical debt' and 'hospital bill payment plan' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Insurance policy changes and healthcare cost announcements trigger 2-3 search surges per quarter.

title: Port Container Volume Surge Recovery Rally
thesis: Sudden week-over-week container volume increases signal restocking demand and manufacturing pickup, creating short-term bullish impulses in industrial and cyclical sectors. Port container surges indicate inventory restocking and supply chain normalization, signaling demand recovery.
data_sources: Port Authority container volume data (TEU) weekly through port_container_volume adapter, excluding PoLA already tracked
signal_logic: If weekly container volume increases 8% from prior week and is above 8-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly container volume increases 8% from prior week and is above 8-week moving average Exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if volume falls back below 8-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Port Authority container volume data (TEU) weekly through port_container_volume adapter, excluding PoLA already tracked via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal restocking and demand cycles produce multiple volume surges per quarter.

schema.idea: Port Container Volume Surge Recovery Rally

data.source: Port Authority container volume data (TEU) weekly through port_container_volume adapter, excluding PoLA already tracked

schema.behavior: Sudden week-over-week container volume increases signal restocking demand and manufacturing pickup, creating short-term bullish impulses in industrial and cyclical sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Port container surges indicate inventory restocking and supply chain normalization, signaling demand recovery.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly container volume increases 8% from prior week and is above 8-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if volume falls back below 8-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal restocking and demand cycles produce multiple volume surges per quarter.

title: News Sentiment Spike In Supply Chain Disruption Headlines
thesis: Surges in negative supply chain headlines correlate with short-term bearish sentiment in industrials and materials, often followed by mean-reversion as markets digest the news. Supply chain disruption headlines create uncertainty and pressure on industrial sector valuations and execution.
data_sources: RSS feed count and sentiment for articles containing 'supply chain disruption' or 'logistics crisis' through rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain disruption terms exceeds prior 4-week average by 60% and sentiment is >60% negative
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain disruption terms exceeds prior 4-week average by 60% and sentiment is >60% negative Exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if RSS count falls below 4-week average for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count and sentiment for articles containing 'supply chain disruption' or 'logistics crisis' through rss_count adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain news cycles produce 2-4 intense spike periods per quarter.

schema.idea: News Sentiment Spike In Supply Chain Disruption Headlines

data.source: RSS feed count and sentiment for articles containing 'supply chain disruption' or 'logistics crisis' through rss_count adapter

schema.behavior: Surges in negative supply chain headlines correlate with short-term bearish sentiment in industrials and materials, often followed by mean-reversion as markets digest the news.

market_impact.rationale: Supply chain disruption headlines create uncertainty and pressure on industrial sector valuations and execution.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain disruption terms exceeds prior 4-week average by 60% and sentiment is >60% negative
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if RSS count falls below 4-week average for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain news cycles produce 2-4 intense spike periods per quarter.
